div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Spain (Bowers), Then in France
div.p: B–446. Your 503, July 18, 1 p.m. Bay has, in the course of his recent visits to Burgos in several conversations with the Franco authorities, dealt with the matter of treatment of American prisoners, and it was suggested to him that questions concerning prisoners be taken up by him with the authorities concerned. Pursuant to this suggestion, we have therefore made our preliminary approach through Bay.

Until the interested American organization offered to pay expenses of repatriation or exchange, it was not possible for us to make any inquiries along those lines, as the Government has not appropriated funds at its disposal for either of those purposes.
If our preliminary approach receives favorable response, the method and details of working out the repatriation or exchange can be taken up.
